title: PROJECT LITERACY - 211info
text: UMPQUA VALLEY DISABILITIES NETWORK PROJECT LITERACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Tutoring and adult literacy program that offers one-on-one and small group instruction in reading, writing, GED preparation, and English as a Second Language (ESL) courses. 736 SE Jackson Street Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Adults with disabilities and adults who identify as having low literacy skills Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 672-6336 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 672-6336 TTY/TTD (541) 440-2882 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information and to schedule an appointment. View website for more information. FEES: None; donations accepted DOCUMENTS: Photo ID, if available LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Douglas County Services: English as a Second Language GED/High School Equivalency Test Instruction Literacy Programs Tutoring Services Other Services Offered At This Location: REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - CENTERS FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. UMPQUA VALLEY DISABILITIES NETWORK
